OpenGit
FeaturesPricingFAQGitHubGet OpenGit
MIT licensed. Open source forever.

A Git client you own.

Pay once, use it forever. Open source, works offline, no telemetry, no account. Your repos never leave your machine.

Buy for $15, onceView source on GitHub

macOS, Windows, and Linux. Linux builds are free.

app screenshotcommit graph, staging, and diff view

Everything a daily driver needs

Built in Rust and React on Tauri. Small, fast, and local. The whole client is designed around doing real Git work without getting in your way.

Commit graph

A fast, readable graph of your history. Branches, merges, and tags laid out the way you picture them.

Staging that makes sense

Stage files, hunks, or single lines. See exactly what goes into every commit before it happens.

Side-by-side diffs

Clean split diffs with syntax awareness, so reviews and comparisons stay easy on the eyes.

Merge conflict resolver

Resolve conflicts inside the app with a clear three-way view. No hunting through conflict markers.

Safety snapshots

Before any destructive operation, OpenGit takes a snapshot. Rebase, reset, force push, all reversible.

Branches, remotes, stashes

Full branch, remote, and stash management from one panel. Create, rename, push, pop, drop.

Azure DevOps, first class

Pull requests, branch policies, and auth for Azure DevOps built in. Rare in Git clients, standard here. GitHub and GitLab work over plain git too.

AI commit messages

Generate commit messages from your staged diff with your own OpenAI key. Optional, off by default.

Local-first, private

Everything runs on your machine. No telemetry, no account, no network calls you did not ask for.

One price. No subscription.

The code is free forever. The official signed builds cost $15, once, and fund the project.

Build from source

Clone the repo and build it yourself. Same app, same features, MIT licensed.

Official builds

Buy OpenGit

Questions, answered

Why sell builds if the code is open source?

Signing certificates, notarization, and update infrastructure cost real money, and building a good Git client takes time. The $15 covers the official, signed, auto-updating installers. The code itself is MIT licensed and always free to build yourself.

What platforms are supported?

macOS (signed and notarized), Windows, and Linux. Linux builds are free. You can build from source on any platform.

Do updates cost extra?

No. The $15 is one time. Official builds auto-update, and every future version is included.

Is there telemetry?

No. OpenGit sends nothing anywhere. No analytics, no crash reporting, no account, no phone-home. The only network traffic is the git operations you run, plus the optional AI feature if you add your own OpenAI key.

OpenGit. MIT licensed, local-first.
